explanations.1: This special comment tells Docker to use a specific version of the Dockerfile syntax, which enables newer features.
explanations.10: The 'set -e -x' command makes the shell exit immediately if any command fails and print each command before running it, which helps catch errors early.
explanations.11: The '-m 0700' flag sets permissions so only the owner (root) can read, write, and enter the .ssh directory for security.
explanations.14: This keeps the container alive indefinitely by running a command that never finishes, otherwise the container would exit immediately after starting.
